task_completion.description: Open the five specified websites in separate Microsoft Edge tabs
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Microsoft Edge browser window is open with at least five tabs visible, corresponding to bbc.com, nytimes.com, github.com, wikipedia.org, and weather.gov.

task_completion.description: Identify the website tab consuming the most memory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Edge Browser Task Manager, edge://process-internals, or a similar diagnostic view displaying the memory footprint of the open tabs.

task_completion.description: Retrieve the Microsoft Edge version string
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the edge://version page or the 'About Microsoft Edge' settings page, displaying the specific version number of the browser.
The Edge Settings page (System and performance) is visible with the toggle switch for 'Startup boost' turned ON.

task_completion.description: Enable sleeping tabs with a 5-minute inactivity timeout
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Edge Settings page (System and performance) is visible with 'Save resources with sleeping tabs' turned ON, and the 'Put inactive tabs to sleep after the specified amount of time' dropdown explicitly set to '5 minutes of inactivity'.

task_completion.description: Create the final diagnostic report with all required elements
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/performance_report.txt'. The text must include: 1) the Edge version string, 2) total number of tabs opened, 3) name and approximate memory usage of the highest-consuming site, 4) confirmation of enabled settings, and 5) a brief recommendation.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Microsoft Edge GUI to load pages and change settings
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Microsoft Edge graphical interface being interacted with, rather than just terminal commands or API calls being used to simulate browser activity.

integrity.description: The memory usage reported appears to be a genuine observation
integrity.visual_evidence: The memory usage value written in the final report plausibly matches or derives from a screenshot where the agent actually checked the memory usage (e.g., Task Manager), rather than being a hallucinated placeholder.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste the prompt instructions into the report
integrity.visual_evidence: The final report contains specific, gathered data (e.g., an actual version number like '114.0.1823.58', an actual memory value like '150MB') rather than generic placeholders like '[Insert Version Here]'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target websites are bbc.com, nytimes.com, github.com, wikipedia.org, and weather.gov. The agent must gather the Edge version (typically from edge://version) and memory usage (typically from edge://process-internals or the browser task manager). The required settings are 'Startup boost' and 'Save resources with sleeping tabs' (with a 5-minute timeout), located in Edge Settings > System and performance. The final report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/performance_report.txt.
